pi_items.source: Verified via environment setup scripts: install_qblade.sh creates /home/ga/Documents/projects directory with ga:ga ownership. Task save location confirmed in task.json description.
Verified via task.json description step 9: 'Create a report file at /home/ga/Documents/projects/ripple_report.txt'. Path is in same directory as project file.
Task description specifies '3-bladed H-Rotor VAWT'. Three-bladed design is standard for H-rotor VAWTs to reduce torque ripple compared to two-blade designs.
Task description: 'Radius=2m'. This is a standard small-to-medium scale turbine design.
Task description: 'Blade Height=3m'. Height to radius ratio of 1.5 is typical for H-rotor VAWTs.
Task description: 'Chord=0.25m'. Chord-to-radius ratio of 0.125 is reasonable for VAWT blade design.

Web search confirmed NACA 0021 is a real, symmetrical airfoil with 21% maximum thickness at 30% chord. Available in UIUC airfoil database (https://m-selig.ae.illinois.edu/ads/coord_database.html) and standard PDAS database. QBlade v0.96.2 can generate NACA 4-digit airfoils dynamically including NACA 0021.

Task description: 'fixed Tip Speed Ratio (TSR) of 2.5'. Web search confirmed TSR 2.5 is a standard operating point for VAWT research and performance studies. Multiple peer-reviewed studies cite TSR 2.5 as optimal or standard condition.
Task description: 'Wind Speed of 10 m/s'. This is a moderate, realistic wind speed for wind turbine simulation and falls in typical operating range.

Task description: 'Re=300,000'. Web search (https://sourceforge.net/p/qblade/discussion/1195932/thread/bd701be8/) confirmed Re=300,000 is a standard Reynolds number for wind turbine airfoil analysis. Multiple studies cite this as representative for small-to-medium turbines (1-5 kW range). XFoil analysis at this Reynolds number is well-validated.

Physics calculation: For VAWT with R=2m, H=3m, c=0.25m, V=10m/s, TSR=2.5: Swept area = 12 m². Power ≈ 2-3 kW for Cp≈0.3-0.4. Angular velocity ω = 12.5 rad/s. Torque = Power/ω ≈ 160-240 Nm. Metadata range 100-300 Nm is physically reasonable. Lower bound of 100 Nm provides safety margin.

Physics calculation: Upper bound of 300 Nm is conservative for this turbine. Accounts for variations in Cp, wind gust effects, and simulation uncertainties. Falls within expected range for 2-3 kW power output.
Physics: TRF = (Qmax - Qmin) / Qavg. For 3-bladed VAWT, torque ripple is lower than 2-bladed designs. Lower bound 0.05 represents optimized smooth operation. Physically plausible.
Physics: Upper bound 1.0 is conservative estimate for worst-case ripple (Qmax ≈ Qavg when Qmin approaches zero). Typical VAWTs operate with TRF in 0.1-0.6 range, so 1.0 is an inclusive upper limit.

privileged_info_summary: This task performs a synthetic VAWT (Vertical Axis Wind Turbine) torque ripple analysis using QBlade v0.96.2 Community Edition running on Linux. The simulation designs a 3-bladed H-rotor with radius 2m, height 3m, and NACA 0021 airfoils (21% thickness symmetrical profile, verified as real airfoil available in UIUC database). The DMS simulation operates at TSR 2.5 and 10 m/s wind speed with Reynolds number 300,000, all standard conditions confirmed in wind turbine research. Expected torque (Q_avg) range of 100-300 Nm is physically consistent with calculated power output (2-3 kW) for this turbine geometry. Torque ripple factor range 0.05-1.0 is appropriate for 3-bladed design, with 0.05 representing smooth operation and 1.0 as conservative upper bound. All metadata values are either directly verified against authoritative sources or confirmed as physically plausible through engineering analysis.
